summary: Keep the mobile-layout browser acceptance assertion intact while giving its initial application mount a bounded budget under Windows runner contention.

externalInputs.purpose: Observed the exact final-main Windows browser-acceptance failure and bounded the correction to its mobile-layout startup assertion.

decisions.decision: Give only the affected mobile-layout test a 60-second enclosing budget, wait up to 15 seconds for the workspace frame to mount, and then independently assert that its data-layout-mode is narrow.
decisions.rationale: The final-main Windows run timed out before finding the narrow frame, and the focused local trace showed navigation could consume about 29 seconds of the old 30-second test budget while the eventual DOM was correct. Separating mount readiness from the layout assertion preserves the behavioral oracle without a retry or an assertion waiver.

testEvidence.criterion: The mobile-web layout acceptance test tolerates a slow initial mount while still proving the complete narrow presentation.
testEvidence.test: Run only the affected Playwright case after adding bounded mount readiness and retaining the narrow-mode, switcher, editor-position, and overflow assertions.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec playwright test tests/e2e/c0-responsive.e2e.ts --grep "uses the complete narrow presentation above the width breakpoint"
red.outcome: RED: the 30-second test timed out after navigation consumed about 29 seconds; the trace's final DOM contained the correct workspace-frame with data-layout-mode=narrow.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec playwright test tests/e2e/c0-responsive.e2e.ts --grep "uses the complete narrow presentation above the width breakpoint"
green.outcome: GREEN: the single affected case passed in 4.3 seconds; 1 test passed with no retry.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
